aids.title: D-R-A-C: Dynamic Array Steps
aids.content: Declare array, Resize when full, Amortized O(1), Copy elements. D-R-A-C reminds you of the lifecycle of every dynamic array enqueue.
aids.forRemembering: The four key steps in dynamic array queue operation

aids.title: Savings Account for Amortized Analysis
aids.content: Think of each enqueue as depositing 3 coins: 1 coin pays for the insertion, and 2 coins go into savings. When a resize happens, the savings account pays for copying all elements. You never go bankrupt because each element contributed enough.
aids.forRemembering: Why amortized cost is O(1) using the accounting method

aids.title: Quarter Rule for Shrinking
aids.content: Half causes Havoc, Quarter is Quiet. Shrinking at 1/2 capacity causes thrashing; shrinking at 1/4 capacity provides a safe buffer zone.
aids.forRemembering: When to shrink a dynamic array to avoid thrashing

aids.title: Geometric Series Proof Shortcut
aids.content: Total copies during n insertions with doubling: n/2 + n/4 + n/8 + ... < n. So total work is at most n (insertions) + n (copies) = 2n, giving amortized cost 2n/n = O(1).
aids.forRemembering: Quick proof that doubling gives amortized O(1)
